page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c1.large is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the ecs.c1 server family. It features 8 dedicated physical CPU cores with 1 thread per core, resulting in 8 vCPUs. The instance is provisioned with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It operates on the KVM hypervisor.

This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external network-attached volumes. It also includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

With a dedicated CPU allocation and a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the ecs.c1.large is structured for compute-heavy workloads that require consistent CPU performance without hyperthreading overhead. The qualitative cost profile reflects a compute-optimized resource density, making it suitable for batch processing, web serving, and distributed analytics where memory footprint is secondary to raw processing power.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c1.large is a compute-optimized virtual server instance built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated physical CPU cores with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio, providing 8 dedicated vCPUs. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, establishing a 2.0 GB per core allocation. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Designed with a dedicated CPU allocation, the ecs.c1.large offers stable processing capabilities for workloads requiring consistent CPU execution. It is suitable for compute-intensive tasks, batch processing, and web serving applications that do not require large memory footprints or local storage.
